title: Production Order
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of Production Orders.
Learn Production Order processing in SAP.
Identify important SAP Production Order Transaction Codes.

introduction.title: What is a Production Order?
introduction.content: A Production Order is an execution document in SAP Production Planning that authorizes the manufacturing of a specific quantity of a material. It contains all the information required for production including materials, operations, work centers, production dates, costs and confirmations.

business_purpose.title: Why is a Production Order Important?
business_purpose.content: A Production Order acts as the official instruction to manufacture a product. It brings together the Bill of Material (BOM), Routing, Work Centers and scheduling information, ensuring production is carried out according to the defined manufacturing process.

integration.purpose: Creates Planned Orders that are converted into Production Orders.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A Production Planner creates a Production Order for manufacturing 1,000 stainless steel coils. SAP automatically copies the BOM and Routing, assigns the appropriate Work Centers, schedules production, issues raw materials, records confirmations and posts Goods Receipt after production is completed.

best_practices: Review BOM before releasing the order.
important_notes: Production Orders are usually generated from Planned Orders created by MRP.
Goods Issue should normally occur before production begins.
Goods Receipt is posted after manufacturing is completed.
Order status should be monitored throughout the production lifecycle.
jsl_notes: Production Orders should be monitored regularly using Production Order reporting tools.
Manufacturing teams should verify production quantities and order status before confirmation.
Follow organization-specific production approval and execution procedures.
